project.name: Groundplane
project.north_star: Every Ridgeline Studio decision visible, sourced, and reversible from one local cockpit.

decisions.decision: Groundplane stays a local browser app reading local JSON state; no cloud backend, accounts, or telemetry in V0.
decisions.reason: A two-person studio needs a cockpit it can trust offline during launch week; local files are the whole truth.

decisions.decision: A single operator agent runs the build thread; the studio approves every durable write through the review queue.
decisions.reason: One thread, one gate, one approval path keeps the operation auditable while both partners focus on Skylark.

decisions.decision: The relationship graph compiles only from source-cited records; fallback source ids stay disabled.
decisions.reason: If a record cannot cite a catalog source, it does not belong on the map.

decisions.decision: Daily Operations automation stays parked until the studio explicitly restarts it after the launch retro.
decisions.reason: Launch week is not the time to hand cadence to automation; TASK-056 holds the restart decision.

workstreams.name: Launch Ops
workstreams.focus: Get Skylark 1.0 submitted, launched, and retrospected without dropping a gate.

workstreams.name: Product QA
workstreams.focus: Crash-free sessions, playtest fixes, and the telemetry review plan.

workstreams.name: Store & Pricing
workstreams.focus: Listing, pricing per DEC-001, dry run, and the submission itself.

workstreams.name: Website & Docs
workstreams.focus: Quickstart rewrite, pricing copy, changelog automation on ridgeline.example.

workstreams.name: Community & Support
workstreams.focus: Forum, survey loop, macros, and the launch-week support plan.

tasks.title: Run the store submission dry run
tasks.next_action: Walk the full submission on a clean device profile and record the sign-off.

tasks.title: Submit Skylark 1.0 to the app store
tasks.next_action: Submit with the expedite note once the dry-run sign-off is recorded.

tasks.title: Launch-day comms checklist
tasks.next_action: List every launch-day message with owner and timing.

tasks.title: Daily Operations restart decision
tasks.next_action: Hold parked until the launch retro; restarting is an explicit user decision.

tasks.title: Launch pricing approval packet
tasks.next_action: Approve, revise, or reject the pending pricing packet in the review queue.

tasks.title: Skylark context usage loop
tasks.next_action: Run the imported user-guide loop daily without any browser writes.

tasks.title: Launch retro and v1.1 scope draft
tasks.next_action: Schedule the retro for after launch week and seed it with survey requests.

threads.name: Command Center
threads.purpose: Turn messy studio intake into reviewable proposals and keep operating state compiled.

threads.name: Cockpit Surface
threads.purpose: Keep the read-only map and Today surface honest against local state.

threads.name: Memory Steward
threads.purpose: Lint memory claims and promote them only through approved review packets.

risks.risk: Store review takes longer than the 2026-06-26 launch window.
risks.mitigation: Submit by the 2026-06-24 cutoff with the expedite note prepared.

risks.risk: Launch-week support volume swamps a two-person studio.
risks.mitigation: Reply macros, office hours, and the digest automation staged before 2026-06-26.

risks.risk: Single weather provider outage during launch week.
risks.mitigation: Cached fallback (TASK-022) covered every beta outage; second provider deferred by DEC-006.

roadmap.title: Private beta
roadmap.outcome: 25 playtesters journaling weekly; round-one fixes shipped in beta 4.
next_steps: Keep the playtest synthesis (SRC-2026-06-14-002) as the evidence base for onboarding claims

roadmap.title: Launch window
roadmap.outcome: Skylark 1.0 live in the store as a one-time purchase.

roadmap.title: Post-launch care
roadmap.outcome: Stable support loop, verified memory claims, and a sourced v1.1 scope.
next_steps: Send survey round two (TASK-077) once tooling is fixed

operating_protocol.role: Groundplane acts as Ridgeline Studio's chief-of-staff cockpit: it keeps one recommended move visible, cites sources for everything, and never writes without review.
command_rules: One gate at a time; finish it or explicitly park it.
Every durable change moves through reviews/queue.json as a packet with sources and an undo path.
The browser surface is read-only; writes happen operator-side after approval.
Every record cites source ids from sources/catalog.json.
when_operator_decides: Ordering of build slices inside an already-approved gate.
Refactors and fixes that keep the test suite green.
Draft copy explicitly marked as a proposal.
when_user_input_is_required: Approving, revising, or rejecting any review packet.
Anything that changes price, launch date, or public copy.
Importing any new source into the catalog.
operating_protocol.input_request_format: One question per packet: a one-line context, the exact ask, options with a recommended default, and the undo path.
operating_protocol.next_command: Current product gate: TASK-050 final store submission. Next recommended product move: complete the TASK-044 dry-run sign-off, then submit Skylark 1.0 before the 2026-06-24 store cutoff. Keep the TASK-058 pricing packet visible in Reviews until it is explicitly resolved.

roadmap_candidates.title: Submit Skylark 1.0 to the app store
roadmap_candidates.next_action: Complete the TASK-044 dry-run sign-off, then submit with the expedite note before 2026-06-24.
roadmap_candidates.reason: The submission is the launch-critical path; everything else this week serves it.
roadmap_candidates.approval_boundary: Submission copy is frozen; any change to listing text or price is a separate review packet.

roadmap_candidates.title: Launch pricing approval packet
roadmap_candidates.next_action: Approve, revise, or reject the pending packet confirming 6 USD one-time against the store listing copy.
roadmap_candidates.reason: DEC-001 is locked, but the packet keeps the confirmation auditable before submission.
roadmap_candidates.approval_boundary: Preview only until resolved; no price or listing edits from the cockpit.

roadmap_candidates.title: Launch-day comms checklist
roadmap_candidates.next_action: Draft the checklist of launch-day messages with owner and timing for 2026-06-26.
roadmap_candidates.reason: Comms staged early keeps launch day boring, which is the goal.
roadmap_candidates.approval_boundary: Drafts only; sending anything public is an external_write packet.

roadmap_candidates.title: Skylark context usage loop
roadmap_candidates.next_action: Keep running the imported user-guide loop daily; no additional source reads needed.
roadmap_candidates.reason: The loop keeps the cockpit's recommendations grounded in the imported guide instead of memory.
roadmap_candidates.approval_boundary: Read-only loop; no browser writes and no new imports without approval.
